title: senecio commutatus var. ovatus
intro: Senecio commutatus var. ovatus, also known as the Oxford ragwort, is a plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its biennial growth habit and yellow daisy-like flowers. This plant has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic and to help with fever reduction, due to its high levels of sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids. The health benefits of Senecio commutatus var. ovatus include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory issues. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, and it can also be used in tinctures and salves, although its use should be carefully considered due to potential toxicity.
